Fire is chemical reaction which may be caused when certain elements interact with one another
Rocks/Dirt aren't elements but some elements can be found within the earth such as gold
Water is not an element itself but is a combination of hydrogen and oxygen
Clouds can generally be any gaseous substance (many of which are elements), but the ones in the sky are made out of water

i find this has so much potential.
I dont think the main plot here will be some sort of racism or prejudice against fire people. Otherwise, they would have emphasized it more in this teaser (like, with more people reacting negatively or Ember hiding/making herself unnoticable). It just seems like Fire being Fire. Not reacting well with other elements. Rather just not approach, lest you get burned (i think the hood/cloak's purpose is not to hide, but to protect the other elementals/herself, since on the train you usually stand close to others).

I feel like this'll be more about adapting and fitting in (not changing to fit in, just find your place in the world). Maybe Wade and Ember adapting to make their friendship (or maybe relationship, who knows) work without hurting eachother. 

I'm honestly very excited.

I am too. Seeing this made me realise that, when you think about it, this concept of a fire and water relationship hasn't really been done properly before in professional media. At least not to the extent we'll get with Elemental. Thinking about it, there is some potential for creative storytelling here, even well beyond the obvious, and I suppose Pixar are the first to realise this and take the chance of fleshing out the concept in a movie script, when everyone else has convinced themselves it's some sort of giant taboo.

I think this movie has a chance to be great if Pixar can fully realise the potential of the concept, and approach it in a way that hasn't been considered before. And even then as long as it's written well it'll be a good movie regardless of concept, since execution matters most imo.

The only one who does smile at her is a child who reaches out at her before being pulled backwards by their guardian, indicating that the child doesn’t know any better that she could technically hurt him and the parent believes she’s dangerous. Really interesting subtly details.
I don't think any of them had an outright negative reaction to seeing her, at least.

I could talk about how this film is visually super pretty, or how this is a teaser trailer that's actually a teaser, but everyone has already talked about those things, so instead I'll mention that the "Element City" station sign has an A113 joke, disguised as element numbers (A H Al -> A Hydrogen Aluminum -> A 1 13)

A113 is used in Pixar and Disney movies as an easter egg because it was the room number used by many of the animators at the California Institute of the Arts. Not element numbers, although that would be very cool and is a good guess
I know the joke behind it, I'm just pointing out that instead of sneaking it in a background or in some text, they've hid it with element numbers

Well, I've become skeptical when it came to recent Pixar movies but I'll give this one a try because I find the premise interesting. Not that the world is inhabited by elementals but the couple being of water and fire. This would make for a very interesting dynamic that we often see in real life with contrasting personalities. So I hope this movie will explore more on that

Imo the setting seems cool but i got super bored of fire and water characters being a couple or something, like yes i get it forbidden love is a cool plot point but its really overdone now

In Ninjago the main "couple" have water and lightning powers which makes more sense as a combo of the powers could actually work (combining elements as a metaphor for relationships?? Woah)

But thats just my opinion

@Hello-bs8dn I can see your point and I agree that this forbidden love between contrasting elements is being overused. Personally I have hopes that this movie approaches it differently. Not as a forbidden romance that has to fight against a biased society but maybe a more realistic approach like "okay, we have contrasting personalities and have to find a way to make this relationship work for both of us." Maybe I'm expecting too much. Tbh Pixar has let me down for a while and I agree with critics who say that Pixar keeps recycling the same premise over and over. So I'm not trying to expect too much from this movie but I won't bash it before watching it either.
@NoxAtlas Completely agree 👍
Word.

I don't understand this idea of water and fire pairings being overused. Aside from fireboy and watergirl (which barely counts because there's no interesting story or themes), the pairing doesn't exist in pop culture media. It's very obvious there is untapped potential for storytelling and exploring themes in this concept because nobody bothered to try fleshing it out before. This is why Pixar greenlit this project, they obviously feel whatever story the director wants to tell works with this concept.

Judging from what the director said, there's more to this movie than just romance and the concept of living elements will be used for more than just having to make a relationship work despite difficult circumstances. I'm excited to see how they work with a concept nobody has really tried before because everyone just decided it was unworthy of a full feature film for some reason.

It's actually really interesting to see different reception to this trailer on different platforms. Here, it's all love and appreciation for the art and the detailed animation, the personality shown within the animation, analysis of the trailer, general praise on the fact it's a trailer and not a spoiler. Whereas Twitter has ppl think this is too basic of a plot premise ('forbidden lovers/friends' - although this is just speculation) and missed opportunities for environment design (e.g. they're elements but they just use regular public transport all in one lump) the things about the character and suggested narrative (e.g. this trailer has ppl side-eying ember and she appears to be the only fire character. having a fire character in world of plant + water could create some really interesting conflicts but ppl are speculating it's going to a cliche plot)

After reading the pitch for the movie and instantly thinking it as just an uninspired fleshing out of Fireboy and Watergirl I am... frankly not surprised at just how boring this film already looks. 
Its stunningly rendered, the lighting the environment the textures of the elements from gas to fire but... it just feels like they remixed so many of their old projects into a "I'm from X group, I'm not like other people, I'm *dangerous*" kind of love story. The character designs also just hurt to look at because while the render for the elemental aspects are stunning... Ember and Wade just feel so... stereotypical and old news. I've seen their paired boy types numerous of times, tiny hourglass loner and Broad torso/tiny legs jock.
This feels like the worldbuilding is gonna be the saving grace and maybe some likable features of "hapless guy falling for gaurded girl" will be endearing to the romantics but... you could do that in any setting really... what makes them being elemental special? I hope the movie answers it because Pixar can make amazing movies but it looks like the balloons are running out of helium at this point.

今日日本の若きアニメボイス(声優)スターのひとりであった嶺内ともみさんが今年限りで声優タレントから完全に引退する事が発表されました…｡私はともみさんの大ファンでは決してないのですが､それでも彼女が演じたキャラクターの中には私が面白く観ていたアニメ(小林さんちのメイドドラゴンとスローフープ)の主要キャラにも出ていて､いずれも良い印象に残るキャラクターでしたのを今でも記憶に残っています｡まだ25歳とこれからの御活躍を期待していた矢先での完全引退…｡彼女自身が決めた人生ですので私自身も尊重して暖かく見守って行きます｡が､それにしてもここ数年の日本の若きアニメ(声優)スター(特に20代前半でそして女性)の引退がとても多くなってしまっている事に本当にとても残念でなりません…｡
その一番の要因は一部の世界中の日本アニメファンの皆様には前々からお判りの通り､日本のアニメ業界は世界の数多いエンタメ業界の中でも残念ながら表の輝かしい質の高さと凄い人気とは裏腹に多くのアニメクリエイターもそうですが声優さん達も過酷で労働かつ収入も万が一の保証も将来の暮らしもとても低いレベルである事で次々と日本のアニメ業界から離職してしまっているケースが多くなってしまっています…｡正直､この状態で改善する事をしなければ数年後には日本アニメ業界は一気に地盤沈下してしまい終焉してしまう恐れも大いに有り得ます…｡
そこでディズニーさんとピクサーさんに是非叶えて欲しいお願いがあります‼︎‼︎‼︎‼︎
どうか出来る限り早急に日本アニメ業界に本格的に進出して､日本でもSONYグループのアニプレックスのディズニーとピクサー版でアニメ事業を大々的に展開して､その沢山儲けたお金を今も苦しんでいる多くの日本のアニメーター､そして声優さん達に多く還元させて救って欲しいです‼︎‼︎‼︎‼︎もうこれを出来るのは世界のディズニーさんとピクサーさんしかいません‼︎‼︎‼︎‼︎それ位に日本アニメ業界の現場の皆様は壊滅的な状態です…｡どうかお願い致します‼︎‼︎‼︎‼︎
最後になりましたが勿論ディズニーさんピクサーさんはこれからも強く応援しております‼︎‼︎‼︎‼︎

#ディズニー　#ピクサー

Haven't seen anything about this movie except this very nebulous teaser trailer and I can already tell it's gonna follow the usual Pixar movie formula. Create a new world with anthropomorphized non-human characters that is identical to human society in every way except physical appearance. Make a few dry jokes/puns about that world's direct analogues to specific things that exist/happen in the real world. Have a main character who's a social outcast (usually due to some glaring on-the-nose physical characteristic). One day they encounter a foil character with whom they form an unlikely bond that then gets tested before being re-formed and reinforced at the end of the movie, but in the process they learn some allegorical moral lesson and become better people (or whatever they are).

One itty bitty thing I noticed is how Ember has a hard time being in a relationship with *every* other element whereas Wade is the opposite. 

Wade is compatible with earth because fertilization. Wade is compatible with wind/air because water creates precipitation/rain. Wade can be with water. Wade CAN be with fire, although it's a harmful relationship for the fire - and if the relationship is too heated he can evaporate. So essentially, if he pushes too hard for her, she'll be harmed and so will he if he stays in her life for too long.

As with Ember, she quite literally is going to have a difficult time being with anyone except for *more* fire. She can't be with wind/air, it'll blow her out. She can't be with earth, she'll burn the plants. She can't be with water, she'll be extinguished- and over time, turn the her partner to steam. The only "good" relationship for her is with more fire- since it spreads.   

Makes me wonder if "compatible" relationships are a thing in the universe, as I wonder if certain elements are only allowed to be with other certain elements, otherwise, chaos.

First guesses. This is probably going to be a slice of life, love story. The 'bad guy,' is probably going to be a terrible ex-friend/boyfriend, and it might also touch on topics of race. In terms of easter eggs, the sign for the subway has the letters, A, H, and Al. H is Hydrogen, Al is Aluminum, and from the topic of elements, I assume that A is Air. I would love to see the cloud and stork from the short, Partly cloudy, make an appearance here, seeing as the cloud people are the same design as the cloud people from that movie. There is a sign in the train saying "H2Woah! Surprisingly wet water!" which is pretty funny. Another states that "Photosynthesis is for everyone." I find it odd that Ember is the only fire elemental we see on the train. Wade is certainly not the only water elemental we see, but he does seem to notice people a lot more than the other ones do at first glance. I like the animation styles here. All in all, looking forward to a good movie.

 I'm just really sad to see that probably the corporate and/or Disney side of Pixar seems to be pushing them towards a more unified style across their movies, ever since inside out I feel like Pixar characters have gotten more and more samey... Now that I’m thinking about it, this movie might be a commentary on disabilities and accessibility rather than something like race.

For 1, Zootopia was Pixar’s race commentary centric movie, and 2, No one seems to be concerned or frightened of Ember.

But someone did point out how basically every other elemental person has accommodations on this train except for fire elementals. And since Ember is the Only Fire elemental we see, both in this trailer and the concept art they’ve released thus far, I think it might be safe to say that fire elementals are much rarer in this world, which points me in the direction of this movie being about systematic exclusion in a sort of nudging you towards disabled people kinda way

Wish it was more stylized and less photorealistic, but at least it looks better than Strange World.

I think the photorealism actually makes it more stylized, by having the characters look like real fire and water, it gives us something we could've never seen if the characters looked flat or less real, it emphasizes the elements themselves rather than having other stylized elements overshadow the actual elements.

@r-giireactions2235 I'm not saying it's bad, but I think I've gotten tired of the same "look" of most animated films. That's why I appreciate the extra effort that went into the art style of "Klaus," "The Mitchells vs. The Machines", "Spiderman: Into the Spiderverse", "The Bad Guys," and the new "Puss in Boots". They're still computer-animated, but they went the extra mile to create something special.

@kentslocum I guess I can understand, I just feel that in some circumstances stylization can become a distraction rather than an aid, and at least for me, I feel with how special the element effects are here, it'd just end up covering them up rather than enhancing them, but that's just my opinion, and maybe you're seeing something that I don't.
@r-giireactions2235 Guess I'm in the minority here, so I'll bow out of the comments and let the movie speak for itself. As long as the story is well-written, the animation style shouldn't matter.

@r-giireactions2235 Thank you for clarifying! Very understandable. I'm clearly in the minority here, so I acknowledge that I am overruled. I just hope the story is strong enough to captivate the audience, since the visuals just don't do it for me.

*Theories ahead* (possibly spoilers?*

One of the things I almost immediately noticed was that the fire girl, Ember, seems to be the only fire-type element in the area, I don’t know if this is just a coincidence or to showcase the other elements and make Ember stand out more. At first, I suspected that maybe fire elements might not be as welcome in the area as fire can be quite destructive, but watching the video, you can see that no one is surprised or frightened by her appearance as she boards the train, leading me to believe that this might just be a coincidence. However, there are a few characters on the train that send her quick looks and nearing the end of the teaser, around when Ember reaches the doors, we can see an earth-type mother pulling away her child as Ember passes them, As Ember walks passed everyone on the train, we get to see exactly how the elements air, earth and water all coexist together and how they complement each other's elements, we cannot see this with Ember or any other fire types. it might be possible that the film will showcase discrimination in this way.

When ember accidentally drops her headphones, she goes to pick them up but flinches when one of the water droplets from the water boy, Wade, hits her (skin?) (fire hand?) it's possible that there aren't, any fire elements being seen not just because they can be harmful to other elements but also themselves e.g if a wind/air type were to send too much wind towards a fire type and ends up blowing them out, or if a water type splashes to much water that results in an injury to a fire type.

This could also open up some theories of conflict that might be shown in the movie, specifically between Ember and Wade, the teaser hints at a potential friendship and/or romantic relationship between these two characters but given their differentiating elements, the main conflict (antagonist) in the film may be the differences that separate them how they navigate their relationship around this conflict. (sorta like zootopia)
